label: plain_text
text:
The
polynomial
2n - 1 has
degree
1. Hence, it is
 an example of a
linear
polynomial. Also, the
difference
between
consecutive
terms
in
the sequence
e of the
 number
c of
squares,
that is, 1, 3, 5, 7, 9... is t
the
constant value 2. Thus, with each stage, the number of squares increases
by 2. The relationship between t
the number of the stage and the number
of square
tiles is a
linear relationship.
